mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in falling corporate revenues and rising recession probabilities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which compounds into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 days in 70% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory utilization, causing short-term equity depreciation as markets anticipate margin compression and inventory drawdowns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of observations when INDPRO declines, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during late-cycle expansions (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold, or the signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, creating immediate upward pressure on equity prices as investors adjust forward earnings models to reflect stronger economic output.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals that higher borrowing costs are choking off manufacturing activity, prompting equity sell-offs as recession fears dominate market pricing.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the conditional signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 during tightening periods.
